story.iWant: to authenticate with Google once during my first export
story.soThat: I can export decks to Google Slides without repeated login prompts

task.title: Create Export Workflow Structure

subtasks.subtask: Create `.slide-builder/workflows/export/` directory
Create `workflow.yaml` with name, description, instructions path
Create `instructions.md` skeleton with workflow steps placeholder
Register `/sb:export` slash command in skill configuration

task.note: Export workflow structure already exists - verify and enhance

subtasks.subtask: Check for `.slide-builder/credentials/google-oauth.json`
Parse JSON and verify structure (access_token, refresh_token, expiry_date)
If valid credentials exist, skip to token validation step
If missing or malformed, trigger OAuth flow
Display OAuth setup guidance with Google Cloud requirements
Prompt user for client credentials or path to credentials JSON
Generate OAuth authorization URL with presentations scope
Open browser to authorization URL
Prompt user to paste authorization code
Exchange code for tokens via Google OAuth token endpoint
Create credentials/ directory if not exists
Save tokens to google-oauth.json with proper structure
Set appropriate file permissions
Verify .gitignore includes credentials/
Display success message
Check token expiry_date vs current time
Trigger proactive refresh if expires within 5 minutes
Trigger refresh if already expired
Refresh flow: request new access_token, update file
If successful, proceed silently
Handle invalid_grant/revoked token errors
Handle network errors
Log refresh failures to status.yaml
Add OAuth check as Step 1 in export instructions
Store valid access_token in workflow context
Halt workflow if OAuth fails
Never display tokens in terminal output
Test first-time flow
Test subsequent export
Test token refresh
Test refresh failure
Verify credentials gitignored
Run Frontend Test Gate checklist

acceptanceCriteria.ac: When user runs `/export` for the first time and no credentials exist, the system guides them through OAuth flow
OAuth flow opens browser for Google authentication
Credentials file is in gitignored directory
Subsequent exports use saved tokens without re-authentication
If refresh fails, user is prompted to re-authenticate

doc.title: Epic 7 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: OAuth Manager handles authentication flow with authenticate(), getValidToken(), isAuthenticated() interface. Token storage schema includes access_token, refresh_token, scope, token_type, expiry_date.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: FR42-47 define export functionality. NFR6-7 specify OAuth 2.0 integration with secure credential storage. NFR11 requires credentials stored securely, not in plain text.

doc.title: Architecture Document
doc.snippet: Credentials stored in gitignored credentials/ directory. ADR-004 specifies Puppeteer for export. ADR-005 mandates user-initiated external integrations. ADR-006 defines gitignore strategy.

doc.title: Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: User authenticates with Google once so they can export decks without repeated login prompts. Foundation for Stories 7.2-7.4.

file.reason: Existing export workflow configuration. Story 7.1 implements OAuth authentication step referenced in this workflow.
Current export instructions with Step 1 placeholder for OAuth. Story 7.1 implements the authentication logic.
Confirms credentials directory is already gitignored per AC7.1.5.
googleapis ^140.0.0 already installed. Use for OAuth 2.0 token exchange.

npm.package: Google Slides API client and OAuth support
Used in Story 7.2 for screenshots, not needed for OAuth

constraints.constraint: OAuth credentials must be stored in gitignored credentials/ directory - never commit tokens
Never display access_token or refresh_token in terminal output
Use OAuth 2.0 with PKCE flow for desktop applications
Request only presentations scope - no drive, email, or other access
Follow BMAD workflow pattern: workflow.yaml + instructions.md
Log all state changes to status.yaml with ISO 8601 timestamps
If OAuth fails, halt workflow with clear guidance
On refresh failure, delete invalid credentials and prompt re-auth
User must complete Google Cloud project setup one-time (enable Slides API, create OAuth credentials)

interface.name: OAuth Manager
MCP Google Workspace Auth
interface.note: Investigate if this can simplify OAuth flow vs raw googleapis

tests.standards: No automated test framework currently configured. Manual testing via Frontend Test Gate checklist. Tests focus on OAuth flow verification: first-time auth, token refresh, refresh failure handling, credentials storage verification. All test scenarios documented in tech-spec-epic-7.md#Test-Strategy.

locations.location: Manual browser testing via Frontend Test Gate

ideas.idea: Test first-time flow: Delete credentials, run /export, verify OAuth guidance appears
Test browser opens: Verify authorization URL opens in default browser
Test scope: Check consent screen shows only "Google Slides" permission
Test storage: Verify google-oauth.json created and gitignored
Test reuse: Run /export second time, verify no OAuth prompt
Test refresh: Modify expiry_date to past, verify silent refresh
Test failure: Use invalid refresh_token, verify re-auth prompt
